name: Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail
name_original: Alcalá de Guadaira desde Sevilla – circular por la Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaira
description: Cycle the easy 15.1-mile Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail, exploring diverse landscapes and historical mills.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Dos Hermanas offers diverse landscapes and a network of cycling paths. The region features routes that traverse low Andalusian plains, often characterized by rice cultivation, and areas with old olive groves. The Guadaíra River and its surroundings provide natural beauty for exploration. This area caters to various skill levels, from easy, paved trails to more challenging routes with elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Dos Hermanas The most popular touring cycling route is Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail, an easy 15.1 miles (24.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This route provides an easy cycling experience through varied landscapes and past historical mills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Corchuela Park and Guadaíra River Loop, a moderate 23.8 miles (38.3 km) path. This route allows cyclists to explore the natural beauty surrounding the Guadaíra River. Local touring cyclists also love the Vadalejos Bajo Mill – Gravel track along the canal loop from Dos Hermanas, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail leading through the Andalusian plains, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Dos Hermanas is defined by Andalusian plains, the Guadaíra River, and old olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, paved trails to more challenging routes. The routes in Dos Hermanas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Dos Hermanas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Dos Hermanas?
A: Dos Hermanas offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 90 options recorded on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences and skill levels, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for touring cycling routes in Dos Hermanas?
A: The routes in Dos Hermanas are categorized by difficulty to help you choose the best fit. You'll find approximately 19 easy routes, 63 moderate routes, and 11 more challenging routes, providing options for beginners to advanced cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Dos Hermanas?
A: The months of February and May are particularly popular for touring cycling in Dos Hermanas, offering favorable weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Dos Hermanas has several easy routes perfect for beginners or family outings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988465166/puerta-verde-de-alcala-de-guadaira-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, offering an easy cycling experience through varied landscapes and past historical mills. It's 15.1 miles (24.2 km) long and mostly paved.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic views or natural beauty?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Dos Hermanas showcase the region's natural beauty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990407001/la-corchuela-park-and-guadaira-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Corchuela Park and Guadaíra River Loop&lt;/a&gt; allows cyclists to explore the scenic surroundings of the Guadaíra River. Other routes traverse areas with old olive groves and offer views of the Andalusian plains.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, you can find several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990407001/la-corchuela-park-and-guadaira-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Corchuela Park and Guadaíra River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21841323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadalejos Bajo Mill – Gravel track along the canal loop from Dos Hermanas&lt;/a&gt; is another great circular trail through the Andalusian plains.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Dos Hermanas?
A: Cycling around Dos Hermanas offers diverse landscapes. You'll encounter low Andalusian plains, often characterized by rice cultivation, and picturesque areas with old olive groves. The Guadaíra River and its surroundings provide natural beauty, and some routes even offer scenic vistas of gorges.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Dos Hermanas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Dos Hermanas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-established network of paths, and the accessibility of routes for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Dos Hermanas, you can explore several historical and cultural attractions. Nearby highlights include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seville Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza of Spain&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in history, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1055590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Alcázar of Seville&lt;/a&gt; and various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are routes with more significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40394143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrajas Tower – Vadalejos Bajo Mill loop from Olivar de Quintos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29.7-mile (47.7 km) route with over 350 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding ride.

Q: Can I find routes that follow canals or rivers?
A: Yes, the region features routes that follow waterways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31442914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal del Bajo Guadalquivir – Canal Domingo loop from Casilla de los pinos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.4-mile (28 km) route that takes you along the canal, offering a unique cycling experience.
tips: Excellent car-free gravel road along the Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir canal
Fortified tower in the Guadaíra river valley

For those who come from Seville to make the route to the dragon bridge, it can be done perfectly with a child. Park the car exactly where the Pablo de Olavide university metro car parks are and from there there is a bike path that connects with the road the canal and enjoy a good walk
The historic fortified mill towers high above the Río Guadaíra

The bridge "Jesús Nazareno" (or puente Romano = Roman bridge) is a bridge in Alcalá de Guadaíra (Sevilla, España) over the river Guadaíra. There is no exact historical data, but it is believed to be of Roman origin

The Fuensanta and La Judía springs fed the wheel of the old mills with their force. And these two mills: de Vadalejos and la Huerta were owned by the Church of San Juan de la Palma in Seville and in the second half of the 19th century they became the property of wealthy people from Alcalá.
Great views to the castle from the park along the Rio Guadaira bike trail

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, as outlined on komoot, is approximately 24.2 km and takes around 1 hour and 44 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. If you're cycling it in one direction, the main cycling route can typically be completed in about two hours.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of unmotorized paths, including dirt and gravel roads, with some paved sections. It follows a 'vía pecuaria' (cattle track) for part of its course, offering a varied and scenic cycling experience.

faqs.question: Is the Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered easy for cycling, making it suitable for a wide range of fitness levels and families. It's designed to be accessible and promotes non-motorized transport.

faqs.question: What historical and natural landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich blend of history and nature. Highlights include the Cerrajas Tower, ancient flour mills like the Mills - Puerta Verde of Alcalá de Guadaíra and the Fortified Mill of El Realaje, and the Jesús Nazareno Bridge / Roman Bridge Alcalá. You can also enjoy views of the Alcalá de Guadaíra Castle and the Lower Guadalquivir Prisoners' Canal.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail connects Alcalá de Guadaíra with the Pablo de Olavide University. Look for parking options within Alcalá de Guadaíra itself, particularly near the start of the 'Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra' sections, which are well-integrated into the town's infrastructure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: To avoid the intense summer heat, the best times to cycle are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities in the Seville region.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a multi-use route, and generally, dogs are permitted. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with other users or wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail. It's a public route developed to promote recreational and sporting activities.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the 'nax' trail, 'Camino Retama', and 'Camino de la Retama', as well as various segments specifically designated as 'Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaira'.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses diverse landscapes including the Campiña del Guadaíra, Parque de Ribera, Los Alcores hills, and the Vega del Guadalquivir. You'll cycle through repopulated pine forests, offering opportunities to observe various bird species and enjoy the natural heritage of the area.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the trail that are not recommended for cycling?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the main Puerta Verde is excellent for cycling, a specific 5.2 km section named 'Ruta de la Puerta Verde' within the larger area is explicitly not recommended for cyclists, being more suited for hiking. It's advisable to check local signage or komoot's detailed map for route variations.
